Subscriber Center
body_html:
TOP NEWS
Win $100,000 to host your own conference
Frontiers’ annual Spotlight Award grants the Editors of an exceptionally active, collaborative, innovative and impactful Research Topic with the resources to organize a conference.
Read more
Frontiers CEO shortlisted for EU prize for Women Innovators 
Kamila Markram has been shortlisted this EU award that aims to showcase female entrepreneurs and boost awareness of the important role and contribution of women in research.
Read more
Frontiers takes a stand for open science and innovation amidst EU copyright reform
Frontiers and other signatories urge European legislators to support a copyright exception that clearly includes all research bodies which have lawful access to the digital content.
Read more
Frontiers for Young Minds – Microbiology Art Gallery
A fun series of artistic interpretations of the Frontiers in Microbiology specialty sections, available for download as a coloring book.
Read more
NEW EDITORS AND RECENT LAUNCHES
New specialty section - Frontiers in Applied Mathematics and Statistics
Frontiers in Applied Mathematics and Statistics launches Dynamical Systems. Led by Axel Hutt, this section will provide a communication channel for researchers interested in the theory, methods and applications of dynamical systems.
Read more
New specialty section - Frontiers in Built Environment
Frontiers in Built Environment launches Urban Science.  Led by Prof. Marina Alberti, this section will bring together urban and data scientists from a multi-disciplinary perspective.
Read more
New specialty section - Frontiers in Materials
Frontiers in Materials launches Functional Ceramics. Led by Prof. Sheikh Akbar, this section will cover ground-breaking advances in design, theory and simulation.
Read more
New specialty section - Frontiers in Medicine
Prof. Marc Struelens will be leading Infectious Diseases – Surveillance, Prevention and Treatment, with a combined editorial board from Frontiers in Medicine and Frontiers in Public Health.
Read more
RESEARCH IN THE NEWS
New York Times: Running as the Thinking Person's Sport
Science Daily:  Deeper than obesity: A majority of people is now overfat
MSN: Exercise could not only help prevent but also aid stroke recovery suggests new study
FRONTIERS SCIENTISTS
Professor Cheng wins innovation award
Prof. Yung-Chi Cheng,  Co-Specialty Chief Editor of specialty section Ethnopharmacology, in Frontiers in Pharmacology , won the Innovation Award at the sixth US-China Health Summit in Xi’an, China for his biotechnology start-up, Yiviva.
Read more
FEATURED RESEARCH TOPICS
Precision Public Health
An exploration of ‘precision’ technologies, across a range of new fields, and their potential impact to advance public health policy and practice.
Read more
SUPPORTING THE COMMUNITY
Recently added eBooks
Seven new eBooks are now freely available to download in Science and Health.
See list
© 2007-2017 Frontiers Media. S.A. All Right Reserved
Your feedback is appreciated at community@frontiersin.org

Frontiers SA - EPFL Innovation Park, Building I 1015 Lausanne . Switzerland
Subscriber Center

body_html:
The latest news from Frontiers
TOP NEWS
Second Frontiers Spotlight Award announced for 2018
The annual US$100,000 Spotlight competition is now open. All Frontiers Research Topics completed in 2017 will be considered, with winners to be announced in March 2018.
Find out how to enter
Frontiers wins GOLD 
Out of 22 nations and 1,400 nominations, Frontiers was recognized with the Gold Stevie Award for Female Entrepreneur of the Year, for our continued efforts to open science.
Read more
Frontiers 2nd largest OA publisher in Germany
A recent study analyses the investment in OA publishing by German research institutions, indicating a growing trend toward full open access and raising questions about hybrid OA journals.
Read more
European Research Council-funded Frontiers eBook added to OAPEN Library
Turn-Taking in Human Communicative Interaction was recently added to the OAPEN Library that works with publishers to build a quality controlled collection of open access books.
Read more
Frontiers for Young Minds launches new section
Understanding Biodiversity  will be led by our Specialty Chief Editor Chelsea Specht. It involves kids in the review process and translates cutting-edge research to help them better understand the biological diversity on our planet.
Read more
Frontiers for Young Minds live review event
The annual Bay Area Science Festival took place last month and our authors faced the difficult challenge of presenting their work for live review to a panel of six young reviewers. 
Read more
RESEARCH IN THE NEWS
New York Times: Brain Benefits of Exercise Diminish After Short Rest
Huffington Post: Why Do Some People Love Sad Music?
English News China: Electrode implants offer new hope for paralysis patients
INTERVIEWS WITH FRONTIERS SCIENTISTS
Building bridges in medical research
Dr Michel Goldman, Field Chief Editor of Frontiers in Medicine discusses an innovative interdisciplinary program to solve healthcare problems and translate the progress of research.
Watch video
Clinical trial for stem-cell transplantation in foetuses
Dr Tippi Mackensie, Guest Associate Editor of Frontiers in Pharmacology discusses a new breakthrough that could help to save babies in the womb from a life-threatening congenital disorder.
Watch video
FEATURED RESEARCH TOPICS
Imagining the future
While it is impossible to accurately predict the future, the exercise of vision-making (think – the discovery of water on the Moon) provides our best opportunity to anticipate and plan for our future needs.
Read more
SUPPORTING THE COMMUNITY
Frontiers eBooks listed in Leipzig University Library
Frontiers eBooks are now listed in the Universitätsbibliothek Leipzig, the second oldest library in Germany that boasts a collection of over 5.5 million volumes and around 6,500 periodicals.
Read more
Publishing agreement with Kassel University
Universität Kassel now holds a Central Invoicing agreement with Frontiers and will be covering Article Publishing Fees for corresponding authors affiliated with the University who wish to publish in any of the Frontiers journals.
Read more
